provisions.title: Prime Minister`s Order #90 SCD on Rrisk Management and Single Window_051206
provisions.content:
KINGDOM OF CAMBODIA
 Nation Region King

Royal Government of Cambodia
No. 90 SCD

 To:
 Senior Minister and Minister of Economy and Finance and Chairman of the Steering
 Committee of Private Sector Development

 And
 Senior Minister and Minister of Commerce and the Chairman of the Sub-Steering
 Committee of Trade Facilitation

EXCELLENCIES:

The government rectangular strategy underscores private sector development as its most
important priority and in order to achieve this strategic target, the government laid out a 12-point
plan which includes the implementation of “the Risk Management Strategy” and “Single
Window” mechanism.

In the spirit to successfully accomplish this reform mechanism as planed, I would propose that
Your Excellencies prepare and take action a number of tasks as respective duties as following:

1 - Senior Minister and Minister of Economy and Finance and Chairman of the Steering
Committee of Private Sector Development must take a leading role to coordinate with other
relevant government ministries – agencies, development partners and private sector to prepare:

 i – Single Administrative Document (SAD) for imports and exports administration, this
must be done by March 2006. It would be a single document that incorporates and contains all
relevant information required by all concerned ministries and agencies for imports and exports
administration. Customs and Excise Department is the leading agency for developing and
managing the implementation of this document, for import and export procedures, which will be
based on ASYCUDA.

 ii – A detailed action plan for implementation of Single Window must be done by March
2006. Then the relevant ministries and agencies have to start implementing this action plan

 1

immediately, especially through a pilot stage at Sihanoukville by December 2006 (please make an
effort to execute the pilot plan by this date).

2 - Senior Minister and Minister of Commerce and the Chairman of the Sub-Steering Committee
of Trade Facilitation must take a leading role to coordinate with relevant ministries–agencies,
development partners and private sector to prepare a “Risk Management Strategy” for import and
export inspections, and it must be done by the end of December 2005 and submit the strategy to
the Council of Ministers for consultation and adoption before the end of January 2006. This Risk
Management Strategy must be based on the international best practice principles as following:

 a – Any decision to inspect consignments must be based on identified risk according to
 administrative guidelines.
 b – The Customs and Excise Department is a single leading agency with responsibility for
 inspecting goods at the international border checkpoints. And if there is a need for more than
 one agency to inspect in accordance to risk identified, this inspection must be carried out as a
 single, joint-agency examination under coordination of the Customs and Excise Department.

 In addition, all necessary administrative guidelines and procedures, as well as inter-agency
 memoranda of understanding specifying all the relevant agencies and their respective roles
 (including CamControl) for implementation of the risk management strategy, must be
 prepared and completely adopted by March 2006 for immediate implementation.

I firmly hope and believe in your high spirit of responsibilities in moving the execution of the
above government reform agenda successfully and developing the private sector to become a true
engine for driving Cambodian economic growth as outlined in the government Rectangular
Strategy.

 Phnom Penh, 06 December 2005

 Prime Minister

 Signed and Sealed

 Hun Sen

 CC:
 - The Council of Ministers
 - Members of the Steering Committee of Private Sector Development
 - Members of the Sub-Steering Committee of Trade Facilitation

 2
